name: Gmail
description: Send, read, and manage emails with labels and threads.

reply_to_thread.description: Reply to an existing email thread

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

thread_id.description: The ID of the thread to reply to

message_id.description: The ID of the message being replied to

to.description: Additional recipients to include in the reply

cc.description: The email addresses to CC in the reply

body_format.description: Format of the reply body content. When set to 'html', the body parameter will be treated as HTML content and the reply will be sent as a rich HTML email with automatic plain text fallback for maximum compatibility. When set to 'text' (default), the body will be sent as plain text. LLMs can use 'html' to generate well-formatted replies with styling, links, tables, headers, lists, and other rich formatting elements. When using 'html' format, NEVER include CSS rule blocks (like

files.description: List of files to attach to the reply

name.description: Name of the file to attach to the reply

contentType.description: The MIME type of the file

content.description: The file content encoded as base64

id.description: The ID of the sent reply

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

ids.description: The IDs of the emails to mark as unread

items.description: The ID of the email to mark as unread

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

ids.description: The IDs of the emails to mark as read

items.description: The ID of the email to mark as read

user_id.description: A user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

messages_total.description: The total number of messages in the user's inbox

threads_total.description: The total number of threads in the user's inbox

history_id.description: The history ID of the user's inbox

email_id.description: The ID of the email to read

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

items.description: Email address of a CC recipient

files.description: List of files to attach to the email

name.description: Name of the file to attach

contentType.description: The MIME type of the file

content.description: The file content encoded as base64

to.description: The email addresses of the recipients

body_format.description: Format of the email body content. When set to 'html', the body parameter will be treated as HTML content and the email will be sent as a rich HTML email with automatic plain text fallback for maximum compatibility. When set to 'text' (default), the body will be sent as plain text. LLMs can use 'html' to generate well-formatted emails with styling, links, tables, headers, lists, and other rich formatting elements. When using 'html' format, NEVER include CSS rule blocks (like

files.description: List of files to attach to the email

name.description: Name of the file to attach

contentType.description: The MIME type of the file

content.description: The file content encoded as base64

read_inbox.description: List emails in the user's inbox, filtered by read status. Results are paginated - use the nextPageToken from a previous response to retrieve the next page.

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

scope.description: Filter emails by read status. 'all' returns all emails, 'unread' returns only unread emails, 'read' returns only emails that have been read.

pageToken.description: An optional parameter: The page token to get the next page of results. Use the nextPageToken from a previous response.

nextPageToken.description: An optional parameter: The page token to get the next page of results (only present if there are more results)

items.description: Email address of a CC recipient

snippet.description: Preview snippet of the email content

read_all_mail.description: List all emails across the entire mailbox regardless of labels, filtered by read status. Results are paginated - use the nextPageToken from a previous response to retrieve the next page.

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

scope.description: Filter emails by read status. 'all' returns all emails, 'unread' returns only unread emails, 'read' returns only emails that have been read.

pageToken.description: An optional parameter: The page token to get the next page of results. Use the nextPageToken from a previous response.

include_spam_trash.description: An optional parameter: Whether to include emails from spam and trash. When false, emails in trash and spam are excluded. When true, returns all emails including those in trash and spam.

nextPageToken.description: An optional parameter: The page token to get the next page of results (only present if there are more results)

items.description: Email address of a CC recipient

snippet.description: Preview snippet of the email content

list_labels.description: List all Gmail labels (folders) available to the user. Can filter by label type (user-created or system labels).

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

label_type.description: Optional filter for label type. 'user' for user-created labels, 'system' for Gmail system labels (like INBOX, SENT, etc.)

messageListVisibility.description: Visibility of the label in the message list

labelListVisibility.description: Visibility of the label in the label list

list_emails_by_label.description: List emails that have specific Gmail labels (folders). By default, only returns emails in the inbox (excludes archived emails) unless include_archived is true or INBOX label is explicitly specified. Supports pagination and can filter by multiple labels. Uses label names (not IDs) for filtering.

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

oneOf.description: Single label name to filter emails by (e.g., 'INBOX', 'SENT', 'Work', 'Personal')
Array of label names - emails must have ALL specified labels (e.g., ['Work', 'STARRED'])

pageToken.description: An optional parameter: The page token to get the next page of results. Use the nextPageToken from a previous response.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of emails to return (default is Gmail API default)

include_archived.description: An optional parameter: Whether to include archived emails. When false (default), only returns emails in the inbox. When true, returns all emails with the specified labels including archived ones.

emails.description: List of email messages with the specified labels

items.description: Email address of a CC recipient

snippet.description: Preview snippet of the email content

nextPageToken.description: An optional parameter: The page token to get the next page of results (only present if there are more results)

add_labels_to_emails.description: Add one or more labels to specified emails. This is equivalent to moving emails to folders or applying tags.

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

message_ids.description: Array of email message IDs to add labels to

label_ids.description: Array of label IDs to add to the emails

remove_labels_from_emails.description: Remove one or more labels from specified emails. This is equivalent to moving emails out of folders or removing tags.

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

message_ids.description: Array of email message IDs to remove labels from

label_ids.description: Array of label IDs to remove from the emails

create_label.description: Create a new custom label (folder) in Gmail. Labels are used to organize and categorize emails.

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

name.description: The display name for the new label. Cannot conflict with existing system labels (INBOX, SENT, etc.)

messageListVisibility.description: Whether to show messages with this label in the message list

labelListVisibility.description: How to display the label in the label list

textColor.description: Optional: Text color for the label as hex string (e.g., #000000). Must be from Gmail's predefined color set.

backgroundColor.description: Optional: Background color for the label as hex string (e.g., #ffffff). Must be from Gmail's predefined color set.

id.description: The unique ID of the created label

name.description: The display name of the label

type.description: The type of label (will be 'user' for custom labels)

messageListVisibility.description: Visibility setting for messages with this label

labelListVisibility.description: Visibility setting for the label in the label list

get_thread_emails.description: Get all emails in a thread, returned as snippets in chronological order

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

thread_id.description: The ID of the thread to retrieve emails from

emails.description: List of email messages in the thread, ordered chronologically

items.description: Email address of a CC recipient

snippet.description: Preview snippet of the email content

archive_emails.description: Archive emails by removing the INBOX label. Archived emails remain accessible but are hidden from the main inbox view.

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

ids.description: The IDs of the emails to archive

items.description: The ID of the email to archive

create_draft.description: Create a new email draft or a draft reply to an existing email. For replies, provide thread_id and message_id - the recipient ('to'), subject, and threading headers will be automatically populated from the original message.

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

thread_id.description: Optional: The thread ID to reply to. When provided with message_id, creates a draft reply instead of a new draft.

message_id.description: Optional: The Gmail message ID to reply to (the 'id' field from the email object, NOT the Message-ID header). Required if thread_id is provided.

to.description: The email addresses of the recipients. For replies, if not provided, defaults to the original sender.

subject.description: The subject of the draft email. For replies, if not provided, automatically prefixes the original subject with 'Re:'

body.description: The body content of the draft email

body_format.description: Format of the email body content. When set to 'html', the body parameter will be treated as HTML content. When set to 'text' (default), the body will be sent as plain text.

files.description: List of files to attach to the draft

name.description: Name of the file to attach

contentType.description: The MIME type of the file

content.description: The file content encoded as base64

id.description: The unique ID of the created draft

message.description: The message content of the draft

update_draft.description: Update an existing email draft with new content, recipients, or attachments. Automatically preserves thread context for reply drafts - the threadId and threading headers (In-Reply-To, References) are maintained so the draft remains associated with its original conversation.

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

draft_id.description: The ID of the draft to update

to.description: The email addresses of the recipients

subject.description: The subject of the draft email

body.description: The body content of the draft email

body_format.description: Format of the email body content. When set to 'html', the body parameter will be treated as HTML content. When set to 'text' (default), the body will be sent as plain text.

files.description: List of files to attach to the draft. Note: This replaces any existing attachments - include all desired files in the update.

name.description: Name of the file to attach

contentType.description: The MIME type of the file

content.description: The file content encoded as base64

id.description: The unique ID of the draft

message.description: The message content of the draft

list_drafts.description: List all drafts in the user's mailbox with pagination support

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

pageToken.description: Page token for pagination. Use the nextPageToken from a previous response to get the next page.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of drafts to return (1-500, default: 100)

q.description: Query to filter drafts using Gmail search syntax (e.g., 'subject:urgent', 'from:example@gmail.com')

includeSpamTrash.description: Include drafts from spam and trash folders

id.description: The unique ID of the draft

nextPageToken.description: Token for the next page of results (only present if there are more results)

get_draft.description: Retrieve a specific draft by ID with full content details

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

draft_id.description: The ID of the draft to retrieve

format.description: The format to return the draft message in

id.description: The unique ID of the draft

items.description: Email address of a CC recipient
Email address of a BCC recipient

files.description: List of files attached to the draft

contentType.description: The MIME type of the file

content.description: The file content encoded as base64

send_draft.description: Send an existing draft to its recipients. The draft will be automatically deleted after sending.

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

draft_id.description: The ID of the draft to send

id.description: The unique ID of the sent message

threadId.description: The thread ID of the sent message

delete_draft.description: Permanently delete a draft from the user's mailbox

user_id.description: The user's email address. Special value 'me' indicates the authenticated user.

draft_id.description: The ID of the draft to delete
